hallucination.rationale: The note contains numerous fabricated details not present in the transcript: wheezing, abdominal pain, numbness in hands and feet, weakness, respiratory rate of 20, oxygen saturation of 98%, pulse of 120, a three-minute walk test, chest X-ray, thyroid results being normal (labs were ordered, not resulted), the patient currently taking a benzodiazepine (explicitly not prescribed), drinking more coffee (he actually cut back), drinking more alcohol, and orthostatic hypotension (transcript says mild orthostatic lightheadedness).
flagged_content: abdominal pain and difficulty with bowel movements
numbness in his hands and feet
able to perform a three-minute walk without stopping
A chest X-ray was performed, and the results were normal
A thyroid function test was performed, and the results were normal
He has also been taking a benzodiazepine for acute episodes of anxiety
he has been drinking more coffee than usual
he has been drinking more alcohol

instruction_following.rationale: The note fabricates information rather than stating 'Not documented' where appropriate, misrepresents the medications section (listing escitalopram as a current medication when it was newly prescribed), and fails to follow template instructions regarding omitting undiscussed systems and using proper formatting.

completeness.rationale: While some key elements are captured (anxiety duration, sleep issues, GI symptoms, escitalopram prescription, CBT referral, follow-up), many important details are missing.
missed_items: wife urging him to seek help and the school play anecdote
teeth grinding and cracked molar/night guard
prior GI workup with normal endoscopy and colonoscopy
Monday morning worsening of GI symptoms
45 minutes to an hour to fall asleep
hand tremor hidden under table in meetings
mother described as always a worrier
detailed medication counseling about side effects
discussion about benzodiazepines and why not first-line
sleep hygiene counseling including screen time
suggestion to loop wife into treatment plan
SNRIs and buspirone discussed as alternatives

template_adherence.rationale: The note follows the general template structure but has significant issues: vital signs are duplicated in two separate sections, Social History appears twice, the Review of Systems is poorly organized with subjective content mixed in, and the Past Medical History section is missing entirely.

entity_marking.rationale: No entity marking was attempted anywhere in the note. No {{drug:...}} or {{condition:...}} syntax was used.

duplication.rationale: Vital signs (BP 138/86, HR 84) are duplicated across the Physical Examination and a separate Vital Signs section, and Social History appears twice with different content.
